warnings: ABAP WRITE statements for output formatting cannot be represented in Snowflake SQL
LOOP AT processing logic is not migrated - only the underlying data query is converted
SAP table structures (VBAK, VBAP, KNA1) assumed to exist in Snowflake with same column names and types
Date literal '20240101' assumes YYYYMMDD format matches target date column type
Confidence capped because material behavior is omitted, simplified, or delegated outside SQL.
assumptions: SAP tables VBAK (Sales Document Header), VBAP (Sales Document Item), and KNA1 (Customer Master) exist in Snowflake
Column names and data types are preserved from SAP to Snowflake
No client (MANDT) filtering required in target system
Date column ERDAT accepts string literal in YYYYMMDD format or will be implicitly converted
Report output logic will be handled by consuming application
conversion_notes: Converted ABAP SELECT statement to Snowflake view preserving all joins and filters
Removed ABAP-specific syntax: tilde notation (~) replaced with dot notation (.)
Removed INTO TABLE clause as not applicable to SQL view
LOOP and WRITE statements represent presentation logic that must be implemented in consuming application
All selected fields are preserved in the view output
